title: Client Health Dashboard - Health Scores
description: Cached client health scores computed from 5 weighted metrics. Created by the /client:health-scan command on first run. Scores are cached for 24 hours before refresh.

Client Name.description: Client company name — primary lookup key for score retrieval. Matches CRM Companies database entries.

Overall Score.description: Composite health score 0-100. Weighted from 5 metrics: Last Contact (0.25), Response Time (0.20), Open Tasks (0.20), Payment Status (0.20), Sentiment (0.15).

Health Status.description: RAG color-coded status derived from Overall Score.

Last Contact Score.description: Score 0-100 for recency of last contact. Exponential decay: 100 at 0 days, ~50 at 14 days, ~0 at 60+ days.

Response Time Score.description: Score 0-100 for average email response time. 100 for same-day, decreasing for longer response times.

Open Tasks Score.description: Score 0-100 for open task completion health. High score = few overdue tasks, low score = many overdue or stale tasks.

Payment Score.description: Score 0-100 for payment status. 100 for all invoices paid on time, decreasing for overdue or partial payments. Reads from P11 Invoice Processor DB.

Sentiment Score.description: Score 0-100 for communication sentiment. Derived from email tone analysis and meeting behavior patterns.

Risk Flags.description: Qualitative risk indicators beyond numeric scores.

Sources Used.description: Which data sources contributed to this health score.

Last Scanned.description: Timestamp of last health score computation. Used for 24h cache TTL — scores older than this are refreshed on next scan.

Notes.description: Additional context or notes about this client's health status. May include recommended actions or recent changes.

notes: The /client:health-scan command searches for this database by name ('Client Health Dashboard - Health Scores') before creating it. Lazy-created on first scan. Cache TTL is 24 hours — entries older than this are refreshed on next scan with --refresh flag or automatically when stale.
